named_technique: CRT residue covering via per-prime coprime witness (BEGL96 Claim 4)
bridge_lemma: For each prime power p^a exactly dividing M, gcd(D)=1 supplies a base d coprime to p; since d is then a unit mod p^a, the residues d^j (j>=k) are purely periodic and include 1, so the additive closure of d^k*B_d is all of Z/p^a. Distinct bases use independent digit patterns, so the Chinese Remainder Theorem recombines the per-prime full coverages into full coverage mod M. The converse is the standard divisibility obstruction.
informal_proof_outline:
Write B_d for the base-d 0/1-digit numbers and S(d,k)=d^k*B_d, so Sigma(D,k)=sum_{d in D} S(d,k). Mod M, B_d is the additive submonoid of (Z/M,+) generated by {d^j mod M : j>=0}, where a residue lying in the eventual cycle of j->d^j has unbounded multiplicity and a pre-period residue has multiplicity one.

CONVERSE (necessity). If gcd(D)=g>1, pick a prime p|g. Then p|d for every d in D, and since k>=1, p divides d^k and hence every element of every S(d,k); so Sigma(D,k) is contained in p*Z and misses every residue not congruent to 0 mod p. Thus full coverage forces gcd(D)=1.

FORWARD (sufficiency). Assume gcd(D)=1. 

Step 1 (coprime base is full, Theorem A). If gcd(d,M)=1 then d is a unit mod M, so j->d^j mod M is purely periodic and returns to d^0=1; hence 1 lies in the cycle with unbounded multiplicity, and the additive closure of {d^j mod M} is the whole cyclic group generated by 1, i.e. all of Z/M. Multiplying by the unit d^k keeps it full, so S(d,k) mod M = Z/M.

Step 2 (per-prime witness). Factor M = prod_p p^{a_p}. For each prime p|M, gcd(D)=1 guarantees some base d_p in D with p not dividing d_p, i.e. gcd(d_p, p^{a_p})=1. By Step 1, the single term S(d_p,k) already covers Z/p^{a_p} on its own.

Step 3 (CRT recombination). Group the primes by the base assigned to them; a base d serves a set of primes S_d, all coprime to d, so d is a unit modulo Q_d := prod_{p in S_d} p^{a_p}, and by Step 1 (applied to the composite modulus Q_d, where d is still a unit) S(d,k) mod Q_d = Z/Q_d. Crucially the choices across DIFFERENT bases are independent: the summand a_d contributed by base d is a separate base-d digit pattern, with no shared-pattern constraint between distinct bases. Hence for any target residue r mod M we may, block by block, choose each a_d to realize the CRT components of r on its block Q_d and set the remaining summands to 0 (0 is in every B_d via the empty sum). Since the blocks Q_d are pairwise coprime and multiply to M, the Chinese Remainder Theorem assembles sum_{d} a_d ≡ r (mod M). Therefore Sigma(D,k) mod M = Z/M for every M and every k>=1.

This proves the iff. It is exactly BEGL96's Claim 4 (residue covering) but for an arbitrary modulus M rather than only the specific D=k!*2^C(k,2)*d^k, removing dependence on the AP-step construction.
candidate_lemmas: Bd_mod_def: B_d mod M is the additive submonoid of Z/M generated by {d^j mod M : j>=0}; cycle residues have unbounded multiplicity, pre-period residues multiplicity one.
theoremA_coprime_full: gcd(d,M)=1 implies B_d mod M = Z/M, because d is a unit so j->d^j is purely periodic, 1 lies in the cycle, and its additive closure is all of Z/M.
unit_scaling: if X mod M = Z/M and u is a unit mod M then (u*X) mod M = Z/M; applied with u=d^k gives S(d,k) mod M = Z/M for gcd(d,M)=1.
necessity_divisibility: if a prime p divides every d in D and k>=1 then Sigma(D,k) is contained in p*Z, missing every nonzero residue mod p; hence full coverage requires gcd(D)=1.
per_prime_witness: gcd(D)=1 implies for every prime p there is d in D with p not dividing d (a base coprime to p).
crt_block_independence: distinct bases in D contribute independent digit patterns, so per-block (pairwise-coprime Q_d) full coverages recombine by CRT to full coverage mod M=prod Q_d.
zero_in_Bd: 0 is an element of B_d for every d (empty Finset of exponents), allowing unused bases to contribute 0.
honest_disclaimer: This lemma is PROVEN by the team (modular's per-prime/CRT argument), independently verified two ways: brute force over 2548 random gcd=1 cases (zero failures) plus maverick's separate subset-sum engine (14 admissible families, k in {1,2,3}, M<200). The role here is ADJUDICATION: Aristotle/Lean should VERIFY the iff, not discover it. One subtlety to watch in formalization: B_d mod M is generally a PROPER (Cantor-like) subset of Z/M when gcd(d,M)>1 (e.g. B_3 mod 9 = {0,1,3,4}); fullness is recovered only after summing over bases with gcd(D)=1 (a parallel single-term 'full subgroup' claim, sumset's Lemma A, is FALSE and must be avoided). The Step 3 CRT recombination is the only delicate point; it is constructive. This is the local/congruence half of BEGL96 only — it does NOT prove cofiniteness, which additionally needs the orthogonal density condition sum 1/(d-1) >= 1.
